commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bb2f29b9a89d1a246ec71bbcdf09bc214ffeb262)
commands.review_summary:
The PR stops passing botOpenId to Feishu mention normalization so inbound content preserves the receiving bot's <at> tag, and updates mention-normalization tests for that behavior.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +21. Total +22 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main strips the receiving bot's own mention from ctx.content, and the PR's p2p path can be traced into tag-prefixed CommandBody parsing. I did not run a live Feishu tenant reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor/maintainer revision plus real Feishu proof; automation cannot supply the missing contributor-side real setup evidence.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve self-mention <at> tag in parseFeishuMessageEvent to fix NO_REPLY in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Summary
Fix the Feishu multi-bot `NO_REPLY` bug: when multiple bots are @mentioned in the same group message, each bot could not see its own mention because `normalizeMentions` was called with `botOpenId` as `botStripId`, which removed the receiving bot's own `<at>` tag from `ctx.content`. The model would then conclude it was not addressed and return `NO_REPLY`.

The fix removes the `botOpenId` argument from the `normalizeMentions` call in `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, preserving all mentions as `<at>` tags. Command detection is unaffected:
- For groups, `normalizeFeishuCommandProbeBody` strips all `<at>` tags before command probing
- For p2p, `hasInlineCommandTokens` matches inline `/cmd` patterns regardless of leading `<at>` tags

Fixes #72504

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu bots in multi-bot group chats return NO_REPLY when @mentioned alongside other bots because `normalizeMentions` strips the receiving bot's own `<at>` tag from the content passed to the LLM.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13.1, Linux x64
- **Code analysis path**: See 5 Whys root cause analysis
- **Test framework**: Vitest

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.helpers.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts --run
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
...
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
...
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  87 passed (87)
```

Multi-bot scenario verified in new test "preserves all bot mentions in multi-bot group chat (#72504)":
```
@_bot_1 @_bot_2 check status
→ '<at user_id="ou_bot">Bot One</at> <at user_id="ou_bot_2">Bot Two</at> check status'
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All mentions are preserved as `<at>` tags in `ctx.content`. Each bot can see its own mention tag alongside other bots' mentions. The model receives the complete mention context and correctly identifies that it was addressed.

**What was not tested**: Live Feishu tenant multi-bot group chat (requires physical Feishu app + multiple bot accounts with valid credentials). The fix was verified at unit-test level and by code-path analysis confirming command detection is unaffected.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 12/12 stripBotMention, 15/15 helpers, 87/87 bot |
| Type Check | ✅ | No new type errors |
| Lint | ✅ | oxfmt check passed |

## Risk checklist
**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` - Bot's own `<at>` tag is now preserved in `ctx.content` (previously stripped). This changes the model-visible message body for the receiving bot.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested
